stories.title: Microsoft’s next-gen quantum chip cuts timeline to useful quantum computing
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is advancing its quantum computing efforts with the announcement of Majorana 2.
Majorana 2 is the next-generation topological quantum chip, succeeding the initial Majorana 1 processor.
The development aims to significantly cut the timeline toward achieving useful quantum computing capabilities.

stories.title: Microsoft’s first advanced reasoning AI is here
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Microsoft unveiled MAI-Thinking-1, its new flagship, advanced reasoning AI model at Build 2026.
This marks a significant internal development milestone for Microsoft, moving beyond initial in-house models.
The announcement coincides with a renegotiation of Microsoft's relationship with OpenAI, suggesting a shift in AI strategy.

stories.title: Microsoft’s Project Solara is an OS for AI agent gadgets
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft unveiled 'Project Solara,' a new operating system specifically designed for AI agent-driven gadgets.
Crucially, Project Solara is built on Android, marking a departure from traditional Windows ecosystems for these new devices.
The announcement showcased concept devices, including a 'Desk concept' and a wearable badge, highlighting the platform's intended use cases.

stories.title: Microsoft announces Scout, an autonomous AI agent built on OpenClaw
stories.author: EvanZhouDev
bullet_points: Microsoft has announced 'Scout,' a new autonomous AI agent integrated into Microsoft 365.
Scout is built upon 'OpenClaw,' suggesting a focus on advanced agentic AI capabilities.
The announcement signals Microsoft's continued push into highly personalized, always-on AI assistants.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging apps to local AI agents running entirely on the user's hardware.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B parameter sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Trump signed an executive order mandating a 'voluntary framework' for AI companies.
The framework requires frontier AI models to be shared with the federal government pre-release.
The stated goals are to promote secure innovation and enhance critical infrastructure cybersecurity.

stories.title: Microsoft Scout is a new AI personal assistant built on OpenClaw
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is launching 'Microsoft Scout,' a new AI personal assistant.
Scout is designed to integrate deeply into Microsoft 365 apps (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive).
It aims to automate business tasks like calendar management, expense reporting, and email drafting.

stories.title: Google’s Phone app will tell you if a scammer is impersonating one of your contacts
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is introducing a new feature to its Phone app to combat AI impersonation scams.
The feature will flag calls that appear to originate from a contact's number but are suspicious.
This aims to provide users with a proactive defense against sophisticated social engineering attacks.

stories.title: Google announces deepfake call detection for Android, new AirDrop device support
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is integrating deepfake call detection into Android, addressing a growing security threat.
The update expands AirDrop functionality, indicating continued focus on ecosystem integration.
The feature drop highlights Google's ongoing push to embed AI and ML capabilities across the Android platform.

stories.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
stories.author: Hyunjung Shim
bullet_points: Identifies 'Perceptual Judgment Bias' in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge, where models favor plausible narratives over visually accurate answers.
The research proposes using controlled visual perturbations to systematically analyze and mitigate this bias.
Addresses a critical limitation in the reliability of current multimodal LLMs when used for automated evaluation.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a predictive visual coding method designed for video processing.
Addresses the inefficiency of current Video MLLMs that encode every frame independently, leading to redundant visual tokens.
Proposes a mechanism to transmit full reference frames only when prediction fails, otherwise using compact inter-frame descriptions.

stories.title: MAI-Code-1-Flash
stories.author: EvanZhouDev
bullet_points: Microsoft is launching seven new MAI models, indicating continued investment in their AI ecosystem.
The announcement highlights the release of 'MAI-Code-1-Flash,' suggesting advancements in code generation or specialized AI tasks.
The availability of a Model Card provides transparency regarding the new models' capabilities and usage.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: I held the next-gen handheld
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Intel faces significant industry headwinds, including layoffs and competitive pressure from AMD and Apple/Qualcomm.
The new Panther Lake laptop chip, manufactured on the 18A process, demonstrated excellent performance.
The story highlights the potential of Intel's next-gen handheld device despite broader company struggles.

stories.title: In a surprise launch, China debuts another big rocket designed for reusability
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: China has unveiled a new, large rocket system.
The design incorporates principles similar to SpaceX's reusable Falcon 9.
The launch represents a significant development in China's space technology ambitions.

stories.title: Slate Auto gets serious about privacy for its bare-bones EV pickup
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Slate Auto's new EV pickup, the Slate Truck, emphasizes privacy.
The vehicle is designed without an embedded modem, contrasting with typical connected cars.
This approach positions the truck as an alternative to highly data-collecting modern vehicles.

stories.title: Impulse Space raises $500 million as orbital maneuvering race heats up
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: Impulse Space secured $500 million in funding.
The funding highlights increasing competition in the orbital maneuvering sector.
The news suggests continued investment and activity in space technology.

stories.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: GitHub Copilot is implementing a new usage-based pricing model for its AI features.
Users are experiencing high consumption rates, with some depleting their monthly AI credit allowance rapidly.
The article focuses on user reactions to the shift in AI service monetization.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces ClinEnv, a new interactive benchmark designed to test LLMs in complex clinical decision-making.
Addresses the limitation of static benchmarks by simulating real inpatient admissions with sequential, uncertain decisions.
Aims to evaluate LLMs as attending physicians in a Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation paradigm.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The research proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' for autonomous robots.
These filters aim to ensure safety in interactive robotics despite human uncertainty (preferences, goals, etc.).
The approach modularizes safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely with minimal efficiency loss.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, specifically the constraints of full-layer granularity and contiguous component selection.
It proposes rethinking the granularity of replacement-based compression by suggesting that redundancy in transformers is not limited to contiguous regions.
The research implies that different parts of the transformer architecture (like Attention vs. FeedForward outputs) have uneven redundancy, suggesting more nuanced compression strategies are needed.

stories.title: Modeling Depth Ambiguity: A Mixture-Density Representation for Flying-Point-Free Depth Estimation
stories.author: Jun Gao
bullet_points: Addresses the 'flying-point' failure mode in depth estimation, a common artifact near object boundaries.
Identifies the root cause as the limitation of assigning a single depth hypothesis to a pixel when it straddles foreground/background surfaces.
Proposes a solution using a Mixture-Density Representation to model depth ambiguity.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: dLLMs' masked language modeling formulation is incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding used in autoregressive (AR) models.
Aims to accelerate dLLM inference, leveraging the speed benefits of diffusion models while integrating efficient decoding techniques.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety alignment degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized on-policy distillation for efficient safety alignment.
Argues that safety modifications should be localized rather than relying on global trade-offs or massive auxiliary data.

stories.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) in Large Language Models (MLLMs) to adapt to new vision-language tasks without forgetting old ones.
It proposes 'ProtoAda,' a method utilizing Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation to manage task learning.
The work aims to mitigate inter-task interference, a common challenge in continual learning, especially when tasks have different response structures.

stories.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
stories.author: Jinyuan Stella Sun
bullet_points: Introduces IntraShuffler, a framework designed for privacy-preserving Heterogeneous Differential Privacy (HDP) in Federated Learning (FL).
Addresses the challenge of non-IID data patterns in FL, which can undermine the effectiveness of standard $\varepsilon$-aware server aggregation.
Allows clients to use individualized privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$) based on specific institutional policies and data sensitivity.

stories.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
stories.author: Haimin Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses safety in interactive robotics, specifically concerning decisions made under human uncertainty (preferences, goals, etc.).
It proposes 'Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters' as a method to ensure safety while maintaining operational efficiency.
The work aims to improve upon traditional safety filters by incorporating mechanisms to handle complex, human-induced uncertainty.

stories.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
stories.author: Wenbin Wu
bullet_points: Investigates potential asset-specific biases within Large Language Models (LLMs) used in financial applications like robo-advisors.
Proposes a three-level audit protocol to test if LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments, using Bitcoin as a case study.
Aims to identify internal representations within LLMs that causally influence these asset preferences and downstream financial decisions.

stories.title: Drifting Preference Optimization for One-Step Generative Models
stories.author: Zhen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of preference finetuning for one-step text-to-image generators.
Introduces Drifting Preference Optimization (DrPO), an online preference-finetuning method.
DrPO is designed specifically for deterministic, single-forward-pass generative models, simplifying deployment.

stories.title: Towards Automated Discovery: A Review of Generative Models, Multimodal Learning and Closed-Loop Workflows in Inverse Materials Design
stories.author: Gian-Marco Rignanese
bullet_points: The paper reviews the shift in materials discovery from simple forward prediction to targeted candidate proposal using inverse materials design.
It surveys recent advancements in generative crystal structure modeling and multimodal learning for crystalline solids.
The review focuses on closed-loop design pipelines that leverage modern generators to learn chemical-structural priors from large databases.

stories.title: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics
stories.author: Bin Dong
bullet_points: Introduces 'Iteris,' an agentic research system designed to advance computational mathematics.
Addresses the gap where current AI research often overlooks the complex needs of mathematical discovery (proofs, numerical experiments, algorithm design).
Leverages recent advancements in LLMs and agentic AI to tackle research-level conjectures in math.

stories.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
stories.author: Jiaqi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AdaCodec, a predictive visual coding method for video.
Addresses the inefficiency of current Video MLLMs which treat each frame independently, leading to redundant visual tokens.
Proposes a system that transmits a full reference frame only when necessary, otherwise using compact inter-frame descriptions.

stories.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
stories.author: May Dongmei Wang
bullet_points: ClinEnv introduces an interactive, multi-stage environment designed to simulate real-world inpatient clinical practice.
It addresses the limitations of static benchmarks by modeling the sequential, uncertain decision-making process of physicians.
The benchmark specifically evaluates Large Language Models (LLMs) in the role of attending physicians over longitudinal patient admissions.

stories.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
stories.author: Giovanni Iacca
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current LLM compression techniques, specifically replacement-based methods.
Existing methods are constrained by full-layer granularity and the requirement for contiguous component selection.
The research proposes rethinking granularity by suggesting redundancy in transformers is not limited to contiguous regions or evenly distributed across Attention and FeedForward outputs.

stories.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
stories.author: Junyi Jessy Li
bullet_points: Introduces HERO'S JOURNEY, a new benchmark specifically designed for testing complex rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks.
The benchmark covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction, allowing for granular testing of AI capabilities.
Evaluations on state-of-the-art LLMs show evidence of rule induction, but highlight current limitations in this area of AI.

stories.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
stories.author: Priyaranjan Pattnayak
bullet_points: Introduces SN-WER, a new metric to address WER overestimation in multilingual ASR.
SN-WER normalizes text by transliterating references and hypotheses into a canonical script before scoring.
The method is training-free and designed specifically for evaluating ASR models in Indic language settings.

stories.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
stories.author: Vlada Rozova
bullet_points: The research addresses the public health challenge of self-harm surveillance, noting limitations in current diagnostic coding.
It proposes using Emergency Department (ED) triage notes as a high-potential data source for early self-harm detection.
The methodology combines traditional machine learning with Large Language Model (LLM)-based screening and evidence extraction.

stories.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces SimSD, a method to apply speculative decoding to Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation: the masked language modeling formulation of dLLMs is incompatible with standard AR speculative decoding.
dLLMs are presented as a promising, faster inference alternative to traditional autoregressive (AR) LLMs.

stories.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
stories.author: Huan Sun
bullet_points: Introduces SkillHarm, a new benchmark for skill-based attacks targeting AI agents.
Addresses the vulnerability of third-party skills within agent workflows, which are often implicitly executed.
Moves beyond single-task evaluations by assessing attacks across the entire skill-use lifecycle.

stories.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
stories.author: Lei Sha
bullet_points: Addresses the 'alignment tax' in LLMs, where safety alignment degrades general capabilities.
Proposes SafeSteer, a method for localized, on-policy distillation for efficient safety alignment.
Argues that safety modifications should be localized due to the sparse nature of safety features in LLM outputs.

stories.title: FigSIM: A Dataset for Fine-grained Suicide Severity and Figurative Language in Suicide Memes
stories.author: Mike Conway
bullet_points: The research introduces FigSIM, a new dataset specifically designed for analyzing suicide memes.
The dataset focuses on fine-grained analysis of suicide severity and figurative language within these memes.
The work addresses the critical need for better content moderation strategies due to the growing presence of potentially harmful suicide memes on social media.

stories.title: MAI-Thinking-1
stories.author: LER0ever
bullet_points: Microsoft is launching seven new MAI models.
The announcement is featured on the Microsoft AI blog.
The context suggests a focus on advanced AI capabilities, possibly related to 'hillclimbing machine' technology.

stories.title: Trump signs downsized AI order after weeks of reversals
stories.author: _alternator_
bullet_points: The story concerns a presidential action (Executive Order) regarding AI innovation and security.
It highlights a political dynamic, noting that the order was 'downsized' after previous reversals.
The news is relevant to policy and industry regulation surrounding advanced AI.

stories.title: How fast is LlamaStash? Overhead, throughput, and a fair comparison with Ollama and LM Studio
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: The article provides a reproducible benchmark comparing LlamaStash's performance.
It tests LlamaStash against established tools like Ollama, LM Studio, and raw llama-server.
The comparison covers overhead and throughput across diverse hardware (AMD APU, Apple Silicon, NVIDIA).

stories.title: Introducing LlamaStash: a zero-overhead, terminal-native llama.cpp launcher
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: LlamaStash is a new tool built in Rust designed to launch local LLMs using llama.cpp.
It offers multiple interfaces, including a fast TUI, CLI, and daemon, alongside an OpenAI-compatible proxy.
The key selling point is its 'zero-overhead' nature, aiming for efficient local LLM deployment.

stories.title: Prompt Engineering is Dead. Long Live Context-as-Code
stories.author: eyalestrin
bullet_points: The article posits a shift away from traditional 'Prompt Engineering' techniques.
It advocates for a new paradigm termed 'Context-as-Code' in interacting with GenAI models.
The discussion frames this as an evolution from the early days of ChatGPT usage.

stories.title: Your AI Agent Isn't Failing Because It Hallucinates — It's Failing Because of Rate Limits
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Production failures for LLM agents in 2026 are predicted to be due to capacity constraints, not reasoning errors (hallucinations).
The article suggests that capacity limitations are a major, yet undemonstrated, failure mode in real-world LLM agent deployment.
It promises to detail capacity-engineering patterns necessary to maintain agent functionality under heavy load.
